title: Setaria adhaerens

intro: Setaria adhaerens, also known as fire drill grass or African fingernail grass, is a type of grassy weed that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, Setaria adhaerens has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
